architecture.summary: Circuit-tracer is a library that identifies causal feature circuits in LLMs by finding the features (neurons, attention heads, latent dimensions in SAEs) responsible for a given output and connecting them into a directed graph. The pipeline integrates methods for circuit discovery, visualization, annotation, and intervention testing. Information flows from model activations through feature attribution and causal analysis to produce circuit graphs, which are then verified through interventions (ablations or edits) and visualized via an integrated interface (Neuronpedia).

biblio.title: Circuit-Tracer: A New Library for Finding Feature Circuits

claims.statement: circuit-tracer provides an integrated pipeline for finding, visualizing, annotating, and performing interventions on feature circuits
circuit-tracer is tested with various model sizes, up to 14B parameters
circuit-tracer aims to solve the challenge that tools for finding, visualizing, and verifying feature circuits are complex and spread across multiple libraries
circuit-tracer is made available to both developers and end users via integration with Neuronpedia, which provides a user-friendly interface
